label: Moods
description: The emotional resonance or vibe felt by the reader during or after the reading experience.

tags.definition: Peaceful, unhurried; the reading experience is serene and undemanding
Frequently funny; lightness and humor punctuate the reading experience throughout
Deliberately disorienting; the reader is meant to feel uncertain or lost
Warm, safe, intimate; like reading by a fire with a hot drink
Bleak or grim in tone; the world of the book offers little comfort
The reading experience produces prolonged sadness or hopelessness
Unsettling in a way that lingers; challenges the reader's sense of safety or normalcy
Fast-paced and energetic; the reader feels propelled forward
A pervasive sense that something terrible is coming; dread without immediate danger
Joyful and optimistic; the dominant feeling is positive and light
Intensely distressing; the reader is put through a difficult emotional experience
The reading experience produces awe, smallness, or a sense of perspective
Motivating and uplifting; the reader feels moved to act or believe
High emotional or narrative pressure sustained throughout
A sustained, gentle sadness; wistful rather than hopeless
Evokes longing for a past time, place, or feeling
The scale, emotion, or density of the book is almost too much to contain
Light, inventive, and fun; the author and reader are in a game together
Produces grief or sorrow; distinct from melancholic in its directness
Produces active fear or fright during reading
Dramatic, juicy, and full of interpersonal entanglements
The reader is held in a state of tension and anticipation
Produces delight, longing, or a pleasurable romantic ache
Produces a rush of excitement or adrenaline
Leaves the reader feeling hopeful, moved, or emotionally restored
Playfully imaginative and fanciful; gently surreal
